location.office_time_required: occasional travel for project or team in-person meetings

secondary_archetype: Technical PM with enterprise/internal tools focus

level_signals: Staff title signals senior IC leadership
Comp range up to $228,700 base in SF Bay Area consistent with Staff-level IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years of product management experience at fast-paced technology companies, with at least 3 years in GTM functions (sales operations, customer support, compliance/risk operations) and/or Corporate Functions (finance operations, legal tech, HR tech)
must_haves.qualifier: at fast-paced technology companies; at least 3 years in GTM or Corporate Functions

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Deep understanding of at least two of the following domains: sales workflows and CRM systems, customer support operations and help desk platforms, compliance/risk operations and fraud detection systems, financial operations and ERP systems, legal operations and contract lifecycle management, HR operations and human capital management systems
Proven ability to build products that serve diverse user personas and use cases within a unified platform strategy
Strong technical fluency with AI/ML concepts, agentic systems, workflow automation, and conversational AI
Proficiency with SQL and ability to extract, analyze, and interpret data independently to inform product decisions
Excellent stakeholder management skills with experience influencing senior leaders and driving cross-functional alignment
Demonstrated success delivering measurable business impact through product innovation and operational efficiency

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with AI-powered sales tools, support automation platforms, compliance/risk management systems, finance tools, legal tech platforms, or HR automation systems
Knowledge of enterprise platforms including Salesforce, Zendesk, Workday, and other enterprise systems

Familiarity with sales methodologies (MEDDPICC, etc.), support best practices (ITIL), operational excellence frameworks, finance best practices (financial close, procurement, FP&A), legal operations (contract management, matter management), and HR processes (recruiting, onboarding, HRIS administration)
Background in building internal tools, operational dashboards, or productivity platforms for business functions
Experience with recommendation systems, predictive analytics, ML-driven insights, document processing, intelligent document understanding, or knowledge management systems
Track record of driving adoption of new products across large, distributed teams

explicitly_excluded: Candidates located in San Francisco, CA; Oakland, CA; San Jose, CA; or surrounding areas are not eligible to be hired for this role

scope.surface: Enterprise AI platform powering GTM functions (Sales, Support, Operations) and Corporate Functions (Finance, Legal, HR) via agentic workflows and automation
scope.business_model: internal enterprise product / platform (B2B internal tooling for Twilio's own GTM and corporate functions)
primary_metrics_inferred: Productivity gains for GTM and Corporate Function users
Cost savings from automation (invoice processing, contract review, recruiting)
Business outcomes / ROI from AI deployments
core_responsibilities: Define and execute the product roadmap for Enterprise AI spanning GTM and Corporate Functions
Build AI-powered solutions across Sales, Support, Operations, Finance, Legal, and HR
Leverage Enterprise AI platform to build agentic solutions using multi-agent architecture, knowledge retrieval, workflow automation, and LLM-powered reasoning
Integrate with enterprise systems including Salesforce, Zendesk, Workday, NetSuite, DocuSign, Sift, and internal Twilio tools
Collaborate with business leaders to translate priorities into product requirements and roadmaps
Drive adoption and enablement across end users
Measure and communicate impact through clear business metrics

compensation.equity_form: equity plan and corporate bonus plan eligibility mentioned

green_flags: Compensation ranges explicitly disclosed across multiple geographies
Explicit application deadline stated (06/27/26) indicating active posting
Clear product surface with diverse, well-scoped use cases across GTM and Corporate Functions
Company encourages applicants with non-traditional backgrounds
Equity and bonus participation explicitly mentioned
red_flags: Excluded from SF Bay Area, Oakland, San Jose despite Bay Area pay band listed — creates geographic inconsistency worth clarifying
Scope is extremely broad (6+ business functions; 15+ named use cases) which could indicate lack of focus or under-resourcing
High buzzword density may signal early-stage or unclear product definition
Visa sponsorship status not stated anywhere in the JD
No mention of team size or reporting structure
